description: Extend the PUT/update backend-to-handler result surface for Slice 4 relationship authorization outcomes and align POST-as-update handling with the same external relationship failure DTO. Update handler/frontend mapping where needed so intentional authorization outcomes do not flow through UnknownFailure, generic exceptions, or string-only surfaces.
acceptance-criteria: UpdateResult explicitly represents relationship-not-authorized, temporary not-implemented, and security-configuration failures for relational PUT.
PUT relationship-not-authorized results carry RelationshipAuthorizationFailure with stored or proposed value-source metadata.
POST-as-update relationship denials continue to carry RelationshipAuthorizationFailure rather than a generic not-authorized string array.
Known-but-not-enabled mixed strategies map to a temporary fail-closed 501-style result, while security-configuration failures map to the canonical 500-style result.
Handlers map the new PUT and POST-as-update result variants without throwing or converting them to UnknownFailure.
steps-to-verify: Review src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.External/Backend/UpdateResult.cs and UpsertResult.cs for explicit Slice 4 variants.

description: Add Slice 4 write authorization planning for EdOrg-only PUT and POST-as-update. The planning layer should produce separate stored-value and proposed-value relationship authorization plans from the same operation-neutral metadata while preserving Slice 2 and Slice 3 fail-closed behavior for unsupported strategy combinations.
acceptance-criteria: PUT planning runs operation-neutral relationship strategy classification before request-dependent reference or descriptor resolution.
POST planning retains the Slice 3 empty-EdOrg-claims short-circuit before create-vs-existing target detection.
POST requests that do not resolve to an existing target continue through the Slice 3 create-new authorization path without adding stored-value authorization.
PUT with empty EdOrg claims preserves normal 404 for a missing target and returns relationship-not-authorized only after an existing target is known.
Known-but-not-enabled People, NamespaceBased, OwnershipBased, and custom view-based strategies fail closed instead of partially authorizing with the EdOrg subset.
NoFurtherAuthorizationRequired remains a no-op and does not force stored or proposed relationship SQL.
Security-configuration failures take precedence over temporary not-implemented staging results.
steps-to-verify: Review RelationalDocumentStoreRepository write preflight code for distinct stored and proposed relationship planning.

description: Refactor the relational write executor path so existing-target PUT and POST-as-update perform stored-value relationship authorization inside the guarded write session before request-dependent reference resolution, descriptor resolution, profile contract validation, merge synthesis, guarded no-op detection, or update DML.
acceptance-criteria: Existing-target PUT observes or locks the target in the write session, authorizes stored root EdOrg values, and stops with 403 on stored authorization failure before reference resolution runs.
Existing-target POST limits pre-stored-authorization target detection to the request resource identity or ReferentialId lookup needed to determine that the document exists.
Confirmed existing-target POST returns stored-value relationship 403 before exposing unrelated request reference, descriptor, profile, or merge failures.
Request-dependent reference and descriptor resolution for existing-target updates starts only after stored-value relationship authorization passes.
Stored-value authorization uses the same write session, transaction, lock, or equivalent ContentVersion guard as later current-state loading and mutation.
Missing or null stored EdOrg values map to stored relationship invalid-data metadata rather than ordinary no-relationship metadata.
Stored authorization failures roll back the write session and leave no persisted write artifacts.
steps-to-verify: Review DefaultRelationalWriteExecutor and RelationalDocumentStoreRepository for stored authorization before ReferenceResolver.ResolveAsync on existing targets.
Inspect unit tests proving reference and profile failures are not surfaced when stored authorization fails.

description: Enable proposed-value relationship authorization for PUT and POST-as-update from the finalized merged root rowset. Reuse the Slice 3 proposed-value SQL and failure payload path, but run it for existing-target updates after stored authorization and after the rowset that would be persisted or compared for guarded no-op has been constructed.
acceptance-criteria: PUT proposed authorization runs when the EdOrg relationship plan has a proposed-value subject whose finalized write value can be produced.
POST-as-update proposed authorization replaces the current existing-resource temporary not-implemented branch for EdOrg-only relationship authorization.
Proposed authorization values are extracted from the finalized merged root row, not raw JSON or an earlier flattening buffer.
Writable-profile PUT and POST-as-update authorize against merged values that preserve profile-hidden stored EdOrg values.
Missing or null finalized proposed EdOrg values are bound as null and map to proposed element-required metadata on OR-group failure.
Stored and proposed checks evaluate the full configured relationship OR group independently, so stored and proposed authorization may succeed through different EdOrg-only strategies.
Proposed authorization is not skipped merely because the finalized value appears unchanged.
steps-to-verify: Review RelationshipAuthorizationProposedValueExtractor and the write executor merge path for PUT and POST-as-update coverage.

description: Implement the Slice 4 response-precedence and guarded-state boundary around stored authorization, proposed authorization, If-Match, guarded no-op, freshness checking, and update persistence.
acceptance-criteria: Stored-value relationship failure returns 403 before If-Match, reference resolution, profile validation, no-op success, or update DML.
After stored authorization passes, proposed-value relationship failure returns 403 before a stale existing-target If-Match 412 is exposed.

After stored authorization passes, request-dependent reference or descriptor failures, profile contract failures, creatability failures, and merge-synthesis failures needed to produce finalized proposed authorization values may surface before a stale If-Match response.
After stored and proposed authorization pass, normal If-Match 412 behavior is preserved before no-op success or DML.
Guarded no-op PUT and POST-as-update cannot return success until all required relationship checks have passed.
Request-dependent reference and descriptor resolution outputs that feed proposed authorization run inside the guarded write boundary or are revalidated under it before proposed authorization.

Authorization, current-state loading, proposed-value construction, If-Match comparison, no-op freshness checking, and mutation share one locked transaction/session or equivalent ContentVersion guard/retry boundary.
A stale guard between authorization and no-op or persistence retries or fails deterministically without returning success against a different stored state.
steps-to-verify: Review DefaultRelationalWriteExecutor ordering around ResolveExecutionStateAsync, guarded reference and descriptor resolution, current-state loading, proposed authorization, If-Match, guarded no-op, and PersistAsync.
Run provider integration tests that cover stored-fail plus stale If-Match, proposed-fail plus stale If-Match, and authorized stale If-Match outcomes.

description: Harden PostgreSQL and SQL Server relationship AUTH1 execution for update checks. Ensure stored and proposed update checks reuse the versioned compact failure-set payload, provider extraction, direct EdOrg claim matching, normal and inverted hierarchy direction, and deterministic parameter binding.
acceptance-criteria: PostgreSQL and SQL Server execute stored-value update authorization through the established single-record relationship authorization SQL path.
PostgreSQL and SQL Server execute proposed-value update authorization before update DML and batch abort through the established AUTH1 mechanism.
AUTH1 parsing preserves emitted check index, strategy ordinal, subject ordinal, and failure kind for stored and proposed update failures.
Normal and inverted EdOrg-only strategies swap Source and Target hierarchy filtering while direct EdOrg claim matching remains directionless.
Stored and proposed update SQL includes direct EdOrg claim-match predicates for EdOrg-to-EdOrg subjects in both normal and inverted relationship strategies.
Token EdOrg IDs are parameterized for both providers and SQL Server keeps scalar-parameter versus dms.BigIntTable behavior.
Proposed authorization parameter names do not collide with write DML parameter names.
steps-to-verify: Review generated PostgreSQL and SQL Server SQL in failing tests or snapshots for AUTH1 payload shape and parameter binding.

description: Add implementation safeguards and assertions that unauthorized PUT and POST-as-update attempts leave the relational store unchanged across document, resource, child, extension, update-tracking, and referential-identity artifacts.
acceptance-criteria: Stored-value denials leave dms.Document content and identity stamps unchanged.
Proposed-value denials leave dms.Document content and identity stamps unchanged.
Unauthorized guarded no-op attempts do not emit success and do not create update-tracking side effects.
Unauthorized update attempts do not modify root, child, extension, dms.ReferentialIdentity, dms.DocumentChangeEvent, or other change-query journal rows.
Assertions do not require global sequences or identity generators to remain unchanged.
Rollback behavior is covered for both AUTH1-denied stored checks and AUTH1-denied proposed checks.
steps-to-verify: Review integration test helper assertions for dms.Document, resource rows, child rows, extension rows, dms.ReferentialIdentity, and dms.DocumentChangeEvent.

description: Add focused unit tests for Slice 4 planning, result mapping, write executor ordering, failure precedence, finalized proposed-value binding, profile-aware update authorization, and AUTH1 failure metadata mapping.
acceptance-criteria: PUT ordering tests prove stored authorization runs before proposed authorization and before persist or guarded no-op.
POST-as-update ordering tests mirror PUT after target resolution.
Tests cover stored-authorized/proposed-unauthorized and stored-unauthorized/proposed-authorized denial cases.
Tests cover empty EdOrg claims behavior separately for PUT and POST.
If-Match tests cover stale etag with proposed denial, stale etag after authorization success, and unauthorized existing target behavior.
Writable-profile tests prove proposed authorization uses finalized merged root values including hidden stored EdOrg values.
Normal and inverted Source/Target metadata is asserted for both stored and proposed checks.
Tests cover direct EdOrg claim matching for stored and proposed EdOrg-to-EdOrg subjects, including inverted strategies.

description: Add narrow PostgreSQL backend integration coverage for EdOrg-only PUT and POST-as-update authorization, including authorized updates, stored-value denials, proposed-value denials, guarded no-op denial, If-Match precedence, inverted strategy behavior, and writable-profile merged-row authorization.
acceptance-criteria: Authorized PUT updates the document and returns success.
Unauthorized stored-value PUT returns relationship 403 and leaves persisted rows and derived artifacts unchanged.
Unauthorized proposed-value PUT returns relationship 403 and leaves persisted rows and derived artifacts unchanged.
Authorized POST-as-update updates the existing document instead of returning the temporary existing-resource not-implemented result.
Unauthorized POST-as-update returns relationship 403 and leaves persisted rows and derived artifacts unchanged.
No-op PUT or POST-as-update with unauthorized stored values returns 403 rather than success.
If-Match precedence coverage proves proposed-value relationship 403 wins over stale 412, and stale 412 is preserved after stored and proposed authorization pass.
Writable-profile PUT and POST-as-update coverage proves hidden stored EdOrg values participate in finalized proposed authorization.
Inverted strategy coverage proves stored and proposed Source/Target filtering are swapped.
steps-to-verify: Inspect failed PostgreSQL test diagnostics for AUTH1 payload mapping and row-diff assertions.

description: Add narrow SQL Server backend integration coverage matching the PostgreSQL Slice 4 authorization scenarios, including provider-specific AUTH1 abort behavior, SQL Server EdOrg claim parameterization, If-Match precedence, inverted strategy behavior, and writable-profile merged-row authorization.
acceptance-criteria: Authorized PUT updates the document on SQL Server.
Stored-value and proposed-value unauthorized PUT attempts return relationship 403 and leave persisted rows and derived artifacts unchanged.
Authorized POST-as-update updates the existing document on SQL Server.
Stored-value and proposed-value unauthorized POST-as-update attempts return relationship 403 and leave persisted rows and derived artifacts unchanged.
No-op PUT or POST-as-update with unauthorized stored values returns 403 rather than success.
SQL Server If-Match precedence coverage proves proposed-value relationship 403 wins over stale 412, and stale 412 is preserved after stored and proposed authorization pass.
SQL Server writable-profile PUT and POST-as-update coverage proves hidden stored EdOrg values participate in finalized proposed authorization.
SQL Server inverted strategy coverage proves stored and proposed Source/Target filtering are swapped.
SQL Server tests cover scalar EdOrg claim parameters and the dms.BigIntTable threshold path where practical.
SQL Server AUTH1 cast-message parsing maps stored-null, proposed-missing, and no-relationship failure kinds.
steps-to-verify: Verify a local SQL Server instance is listening on the configured test port or start the temporary container documented in AGENTS.md.

description: Add focused DMS E2E coverage using real claim-set and token wiring for relational EdOrg-only PUT and POST-as-update behavior, keeping final ProblemDetails wording expectations limited to the transitional Slice 4 surface.
acceptance-criteria: Relational-backend E2E coverage proves authorized PUT succeeds with a real RelationshipsWithEdOrgsOnly claim set.
Relational-backend E2E coverage proves unauthorized PUT returns 403 before mutation.
POST-as-update E2E coverage is added when existing fixture setup can create the target identity without broad scenario churn.
Scenarios are tagged with relational-backend and do not enable People, NamespaceBased, OwnershipBased, or custom view-based authorization expectations.
E2E assertions avoid exact final Slice 6 ProblemDetails wording while still checking the authorization status and no-mutation behavior.
steps-to-verify: Verify the DMS container uses the relational backend with docker inspect dms-local-dms-1 --format '{{range .Config.Env}}{{println .}}{{end}}' | rg 'UseRelationalBackend|Datastore|QueryHandler'.

From src/dms/tests/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.E2E, run env -u NODE_OPTIONS dotnet test EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.E2E.csproj --configuration Release --filter "Category=relational-backend".
